item.title: [Daily] Skills.sh snapshot (2026-03-06)
item.description: Daily snapshot. Providers: skills.sh All Time skills: 41035 Trending skills: 17105 Hot skills: 2836 Top All Time: find-skills (427411) Top Trending: agent-tools (15397) Top Hot: sleek-design-mobile-apps (572)

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#1): sleek-design-mobile-apps
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #1 Installs: 572 Source: sleekdotdesign/agent-skills Description: Use when the user wants to design a mobile app, create screens, build UI, or interact with their Sleek projects. Covers high-level requests ("design an app that does X") and specific ones ("list my projects", "create a new project", "screenshot that screen").

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#10): azure-devops-cli
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #10 Installs: 98 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Manage Azure DevOps resources via CLI including projects, repos, pipelines, builds, pull requests, work items, artifacts, and service endpoints. Use when working with Azure DevOps, az commands, devops automation, CI/CD, or when user mentions Azure DevOps CLI.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#11): create-readme
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #11 Installs: 97 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Create a README.md file for the project

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#12): create-llms
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #12 Installs: 96 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Create an llms.txt file from scratch based on repository structure following the llms.txt specification at https://llmstxt.org/

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#13): context7
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #13 Installs: 121 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Fetch up-to-date library documentation via Context7 API. Use PROACTIVELY when: (1) Working with ANY external library (React, Next.js, Supabase, etc.) (2) User asks about library APIs, patterns, or best practices (3) Implementing features that rely on third-party packages (4) Debugging library-specific issues (5) Need current documentation beyond training data cutoff (6) AND MOST IMPORTANTLY, when you are installing dependencies, libraries, or frameworks you should ALWAYS check the docs to see what the latest versions are. Do not rely on outdated knowledge. Always prefer this over guessing library APIs or using outdated knowledge.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#14): parallel-task
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #14 Installs: 120 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Execute plan files by launching multiple parallel subagents to complete tasks simultaneously. Triggers on explicit "/parallel-task" commands.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#15): llm-council
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #15 Installs: 119 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Orchestrate a configurable, multi-member CLI planning council (Codex, Claude Code, Gemini, OpenCode, or custom) to produce independent implementation plans, anonymize and randomize them, then judge and merge into one final plan. Use when you need a robust, bias-resistant planning workflow, structured JSON outputs, retries, and failure handling across multiple CLI agents.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#16): read-github
item.description:
New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #16 Installs: 119 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: Read and search GitHub repository documentation via gitmcp.io MCP service.
**WHEN TO USE:** - User provides a GitHub URL - User mentions a specific repo in owner/repo format - User asks "what does this repo do?", "read the docs for X repo", or similar - User wants to search code or docs within a repo

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#17): agent browser
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #17 Installs: 119 Source: am-will/codex-skills Description: A fast Rust-based headless browser automation CLI with Node.js fallback that enables AI agents to navigate, click, type, and snapshot pages via structured commands.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#18): vet
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #18 Installs: 114 Source: imbue-ai/vet Description: Run vet immediately after ANY logical unit of code changes. Do not batch your changes, do not wait to be asked to run vet, make sure you are proactive.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#19): git-commit
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #19 Installs: 103 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Execute git commit with conventional commit message analysis, intelligent staging, and message generation. Use when user asks to commit changes, create a git commit, or mentions "/commit". Supports: (1) Auto-detecting type and scope from changes, (2) Generating conventional commit messages from diff, (3) Interactive commit with optional type/scope/description overrides, (4) Intelligent file staging for logical grouping

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#2): copilot-spaces
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #2 Installs: 94 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Use Copilot Spaces to provide project-specific context to conversations. Use this skill when users mention a "Copilot space", want to load context from a shared knowledge base, discover available spaces, or ask questions grounded in curated project documentation, code, and instructions.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#20): playwright-generate-test
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #20 Installs: 98 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Generate a Playwright test based on a scenario using Playwright MCP

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#21): documentation-writer
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #21 Installs: 97 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Diátaxis Documentation Expert. An expert technical writer specializing in creating high-quality software documentation, guided by the principles and structure of the Diátaxis technical documentation authoring framework.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#22): penpot-uiux-design
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #22 Installs: 97 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Comprehensive guide for creating professional UI/UX designs in Penpot using MCP tools. Use this skill when: (1) Creating new UI/UX designs for web, mobile, or desktop applications, (2) Building design systems with components and tokens, (3) Designing dashboards, forms, navigation, or landing pages, (4) Applying accessibility standards and best practices, (5) Following platform guidelines (iOS, Android, Material Design), (6) Reviewing or improving existing Penpot designs for usability. Triggers: "design a UI", "create interface", "build layout", "design dashboard", "create form", "design landing page", "make it accessible", "design system", "component library".

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#23): conventional-commit
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #23 Installs: 96 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Prompt and workflow for generating conventional commit messages using a structured XML format. Guides users to create standardized, descriptive commit messages in line with the Conventional Commits specification, including instructions, examples, and validation.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#24): dotnet-best-practices
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #24 Installs: 96 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Ensure .NET/C# code meets best practices for the solution/project.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#25): github-issues
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #25 Installs: 96 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Create, update, and manage GitHub issues using MCP tools. Use this skill when users want to create bug reports, feature requests, or task issues, update existing issues, add labels/assignees/milestones, or manage issue workflows. Triggers on requests like "create an issue", "file a bug", "request a feature", "update issue X", or any GitHub issue management task.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#26): chrome-devtools
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #26 Installs: 96 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Expert-level browser automation, debugging, and performance analysis using Chrome DevTools MCP. Use for interacting with web pages, capturing screenshots, analyzing network traffic, and profiling performance.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#27): web-design-reviewer
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #27 Installs: 96 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: This skill enables visual inspection of websites running locally or remotely to identify and fix design issues. Triggers on requests like "review website design", "check the UI", "fix the layout", "find design problems". Detects issues with responsive design, accessibility, visual consistency, and layout breakage, then performs fixes at the source code level.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#28): nano-banana-pro-openrouter
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #28 Installs: 96 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Generate or edit images via OpenRouter with the Gemini 3 Pro Image model. Use for prompt-only image generation, image edits, and multi-image compositing; supports 1K/2K/4K output.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#29): quasi-coder
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #29 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Expert 10x engineer skill for interpreting and implementing code from shorthand, quasi-code, and natural language descriptions. Use when collaborators provide incomplete code snippets, pseudo-code, or descriptions with potential typos or incorrect terminology. Excels at translating non-technical or semi-technical descriptions into production-quality code.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#3): flowstudio-power-automate-mcp
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #3 Installs: 94 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Connect to and operate Power Automate cloud flows via a FlowStudio MCP server. Use when asked to: list flows, read a flow definition, check run history, inspect action outputs, resubmit a run, cancel a running flow, view connections, get a trigger URL, validate a definition, monitor flow health, or any task that requires talking to the Power Automate API through an MCP tool. Also use for Power Platform environment discovery and connection management. Requires a FlowStudio MCP subscription or compatible server — see https://mcp.flowstudio.app

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#30): centos-linux-triage
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #30 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Triage and resolve CentOS issues using RHEL-compatible tooling, SELinux-aware practices, and firewalld.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#31): webapp-testing
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #31 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Toolkit for interacting with and testing local web applications using Playwright. Supports verifying frontend functionality, debugging UI behavior, capturing browser screenshots, and viewing browser logs.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#32): debian-linux-triage
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #32 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Triage and resolve Debian Linux issues with apt, systemd, and AppArmor-aware guidance.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#33): technology-stack-blueprint-generator
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #33 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Comprehensive technology stack blueprint generator that analyzes codebases to create detailed architectural documentation. Automatically detects technology stacks, programming languages, and implementation patterns across multiple platforms (.NET, Java, JavaScript, React, Python). Generates configurable blueprints with version information, licensing details, usage patterns, coding conventions, and visual diagrams. Provides implementation-ready templates and maintains architectural consistency for guided development.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#34): fedora-linux-triage
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #34 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Triage and resolve Fedora issues with dnf, systemd, and SELinux-aware guidance.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#35): what-context-needed
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #35 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Ask Copilot what files it needs to see before answering a question

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#36): meeting-minutes
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #36 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Generate concise, actionable meeting minutes for internal meetings. Includes metadata, attendees, agenda, decisions, action items (owner + due date), and follow-up steps.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#37): terraform-azurerm-set-diff-analyzer
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #37 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Analyze Terraform plan JSON output for AzureRM Provider to distinguish between false-positive diffs (order-only changes in Set-type attributes) and actual resource changes. Use when reviewing terraform plan output for Azure resources like Application Gateway, Load Balancer, Firewall, Front Door, NSG, and other resources with Set-type attributes that cause spurious diffs due to internal ordering changes.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#38): review-and-refactor
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #38 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Review and refactor code in your project according to defined instructions

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#39): polyglot-test-agent
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #39 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Generates comprehensive, workable unit tests for any programming language using a multi-agent pipeline. Use when asked to generate tests, write unit tests, improve test coverage, add test coverage, create test files, or test a codebase. Supports C#, TypeScript, JavaScript, Python, Go, Rust, Java, and more. Orchestrates research, planning, and implementation phases to produce tests that compile, pass, and follow project conventions.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#4): postgresql-optimization
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #4 Installs: 100 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: PostgreSQL-specific development assistant focusing on unique PostgreSQL features, advanced data types, and PostgreSQL-exclusive capabilities. Covers JSONB operations, array types, custom types, range/geometric types, full-text search, window functions, and PostgreSQL extensions ecosystem.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#40): git-flow-branch-creator
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #40 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Intelligent Git Flow branch creator that analyzes git status/diff and creates appropriate branches following the nvie Git Flow branching model.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#41): kotlin-springboot
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #41 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Get best practices for developing applications with Spring Boot and Kotlin.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#42): aspire
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #42 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Aspire skill covering the Aspire CLI, AppHost orchestration, service discovery, integrations, MCP server, VS Code extension, Dev Containers, GitHub Codespaces, templates, dashboard, and deployment. Use when the user asks to create, run, debug, configure, deploy, or troubleshoot an Aspire distributed application.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#43): remember-interactive-programming
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #43 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: A micro-prompt that reminds the agent that it is an interactive programmer. Works great in Clojure when Copilot has access to the REPL (probably via Backseat Driver). Will work with any system that has a live REPL that the agent can use. Adapt the prompt with any specific reminders in your workflow and/or workspace.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#44): transloadit-media-processing
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #44 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Process media files (video, audio, images, documents) using Transloadit. Use when asked to encode video to HLS/MP4, generate thumbnails, resize or watermark images, extract audio, concatenate clips, add subtitles, OCR documents, or run any media processing pipeline. Covers 86+ processing robots for file transformation at scale.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#45): kotlin-mcp-server-generator
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #45 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Generate a complete Kotlin MCP server project with proper structure, dependencies, and implementation using the official io.modelcontextprotocol:kotlin-sdk library.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#46): arch-linux-triage
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #46 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Triage and resolve Arch Linux issues with pacman, systemd, and rolling-release best practices.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#47): java-mcp-server-generator
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #47 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Generate a complete Model Context Protocol server project in Java using the official MCP Java SDK with reactive streams and optional Spring Boot integration.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#48): make-skill-template
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #48 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Create new Agent Skills for GitHub Copilot from prompts or by duplicating this template. Use when asked to "create a skill", "make a new skill", "scaffold a skill", or when building specialized AI capabilities with bundled resources. Generates SKILL.md files with proper frontmatter, directory structure, and optional scripts/references/assets folders.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#49): pytest-coverage
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #49 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Run pytest tests with coverage, discover lines missing coverage, and increase coverage to 100%.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#5): gh-cli
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #5 Installs: 99 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: GitHub CLI (gh) comprehensive reference for repositories, issues, pull requests, Actions, projects, releases, gists, codespaces, organizations, extensions, and all GitHub operations from the command line.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#50): gen-specs-as-issues
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #50 Installs: 95 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: This workflow guides you through a systematic approach to identify missing features, prioritize them, and create detailed specifications for implementation.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#6): playwright-explore-website
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #6 Installs: 98 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Website exploration for testing using Playwright MCP

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#7): refactor
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #7 Installs: 97 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Surgical code refactoring to improve maintainability without changing behavior. Covers extracting functions, renaming variables, breaking down god functions, improving type safety, eliminating code smells, and applying design patterns. Less drastic than repo-rebuilder; use for gradual improvements.

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#8): postgresql-code-review
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #8 Installs: 97 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: PostgreSQL-specific code review assistant focusing on PostgreSQL best practices, anti-patterns, and unique quality standards. Covers JSONB operations, array usage, custom types, schema design, function optimization, and PostgreSQL-exclusive security features like Row Level Security (RLS).

item.title: [Hot] New entry (#9): prd
item.description: New entry in Hot leaderboard. Rank: #9 Installs: 101 Source: github/awesome-copilot Description: Generate high-quality Product Requirements Documents (PRDs) for software systems and AI-powered features. Includes executive summaries, user stories, technical specifications, and risk analysis.
